title: Selliguea membranacea
taxonomy: Selliguea membranacea is classified as a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It belongs to the genus Selliguea, which is a group of vascular plants commonly known as 'iron ferns' or 'hard ferns'. The species Selliguea membranacea is native to tropical Asia, and its classification is further supported by its unique leaf morphology and distribution. Within the family Polypodiaceae, Selliguea membranacea is grouped with other species of ferns that share similar characteristics, such as the presence of pinnate leaves. At the kingdom level, Selliguea membranacea is classified as a part of the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Selliguea membranacea, also known as the umbrella fern, has been used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. Its leaves are often used to treat fever and other symptoms associated with the common cold. In some cultures, the plant is used as a natural remedy for digestive issues and to reduce inflammation. The plant's antiseptic and antibacterial properties are also believed to aid in wound healing and preventing infection. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Selliguea membranacea for medicinal purposes.

It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. intro: Selliguea membranacea, also known as Selaginella, is a species of plant belonging to the family Selaginellaceae, a group of spore-producing plants that resemble ferns and horsetails. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and infections.
